title: Stuckenia × fennica
taxonomy: Stuckenia × fennica is a hybrid of the Stuckenia pectinata and Stuckenia filiformis species, both of which belong to the Potamogetonaceae family. It is classified as a subgenus of the genus Stuckenia. The genus Stuckenia is further classified under the family Potamogetonaceae. This classification places Stuckenia × fennica within the order Alismatales and the class Liliopsida. The kingdom to which Stuckenia × fennica belongs is Plantae.

It may also have antioxidant effects. Some studies have looked into its potential as an antimicrobial agent. However, more research is needed to confirm these uses. The plant's medicinal properties are still being studied and are not yet widely established.

However, as an aquatic plant, it is likely that excessive exposure to its leaves or roots could cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals.
